channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What is Bluesky, the 'up-and-coming' social platform seen as X alternative
item.description: Bluesky, a new social platform garnering millions of users, is now competing with top networks like X and Truth Social. Jack Dorsey, a former CEO at Twitter, created the site in 2019 to be “a protocol for public conversation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Artificial intelligence changes across the US
item.description: As more companies implement artificial intelligence for daily tasks, some industry leaders are working to push back against certain aspects of the technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who had bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Joshua trees are in peril. California has a plan to save them
item.description: The California Department of Fish and Wildlife has released a new plan to protect the state’s iconic Joshua trees, which are imperiled by wildfires, human development and climate change.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon’s VP explains how to protect yourself from holiday impersonation scams
item.description: Amazon becomes a prime target of scammers during the holiday shopping season trying to steal Social Security numbers, bank information and Amazon credentials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Copilot Studio Agents get a major upgrade via Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 put an end to Microsoft’s dalliance with chatbots. Not because the company is walking away from natural language interfaces, but because it’s taking what it learned with its first Copilots and refocusing on tangible productivity gains from large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American Airlines is expanding its airport ‘gate lice’ crackdown
item.description: American Airlines is cracking down on “gate lice” ahead of what’s expected to be a record-setting Thanksgiving travel season. The company is expanding new technology to more than 100 airports in hopes of keeping passengers from attempting to board a flight before their assigned group is called.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: How Biden’s AI tech could play a role in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration—among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to he
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Coast Guard adds new cyber requirements for ship-to-shore cranes
item.description: The directive builds upon a mandate issued in February — the public notice doesn’t disclose specifics of the new requirements. The post Coast Guard adds new cyber requirements for ship-to-shore cranes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Kai Trump shows Elon Musk awkwardly tutoring Trump in rocket science
item.description: While Trump's aspiring influencer granddaughter happily shared video of her joining him at Musk's latest SpaceX launch, she also revealed moments of the president-elect looking 'out of depth' and 'intimidated.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Podcasters in the White House Briefing Room: A New Era?
item.description: Will Cain's suggestion sparks debate about social media influencers and independent reporters joining the briefing room.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: TikTok rolling out age restrictions on beauty filters amid mental health concerns
item.description: TikTok announced Tuesday it will restrict the use of some appearance effects for users under 18 after teens and parents in a new study expressed concerns about the impact of beauty filters.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Starbucks and other retailers hit with ongoing ransomware attack on software provider
item.description: Major supply-chain software provider Blue Yonder is working to restore its systems after a ransomware attack hit the Panasonic-owned firm last week. Blue Yonder, which counts Starbucks, major U.K.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Majority of social media influencers share information without verifying its accuracy: UN report
item.description: The majority of social media influencers share information with their followers without verifying its accuracy, according to a new U.N. report that was released on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: AI PCs for federal: The future of edge computing
item.description: How AI PCs supplemented by extreme search capabilities will enable federal employees at the edge to leverage more data to make better decisions, faster. The post AI PCs for federal: The future of edge computing first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Influencer houses are dead. Welcome to the production studio era
item.description: Influencers today look a lot more like Hollywood executives than ragtag social media users. And they’re buying up the real estate to prove it. A slew of creators have been buying plots of studio land to film and edit their content.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The biggest IT threat? That seemingly innocuous web browser
item.description: For decades, enterprises have allowed their workers to use whatever free browser they wanted to access the most sensitive files possible. CIOs believed that security software in the environment — such as endpoint security apps or supposedly secure web gateways — would deliver any needed protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Is Outlook down? Thousands of Microsoft 365 users report outage issues
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Thousands of Microsoft 365 customers worldwide reported having issues with services like Outlook and Teams on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cerebras claims record in molecular dynamics simulations, says it’s 748x faster than Frontier supercomputer
item.description: Cerebras Systems, the maker of an AI “chip” the size of a pizza box, is making some impressive claims about its AI processing performance. At the recent Supercomputing 24 show, Cerebras announced a breakthrough in molecular dynamics simulations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Roblox gives parents more control over kids' experience
item.description: Roblox, which has been downloaded millions of times, announced sweeping and restrictive settings for minor safety.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation
